comments.author: Claude
comments.body: Broaden scope: the tracker .gitignore also omits .graph-cache.json (the graph/deps compile cache), observed as an untracked file in the .tickets-tracker worktree after running `rebar deps`/`show` in this same non-interactive environment (Python 3.11.15, bash 5.2.21, Linux 6.18.5; rebar run via PYTHONPATH=src). Same root cause as the write-lock files: _GITIGNORE in src/rebar/_commands/init.py does not cover all runtime artifacts. Fix should ignore .ticket-write.lock, .ticket-write.lock.d/, AND .graph-cache.json (ideally derive the cache/lock names from their defining constants so the ignore list cannot drift).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: ROOT CAUSE (confirmed + scoped): seeded _GITIGNORE omitted .ticket-write.lock and .graph-cache.json; the flock lock file is intentionally never unlinked on release, so both surface untracked. (.ticket-write.lock.d does NOT normally surface — it is rmdir'd on release — so the steady noise is just those two.) FIX (branch worktree-civil-marlin-flare, commit f3d81f94): derive _GITIGNORE from the WRITE_LOCK_NAME/MKDIR_LOCK_NAME/_GRAPH_CACHE_FILE constants (no drift); _commit_gitignore migrates existing trackers (idempotent), called from the already-initialized init path too. Pending PR/merge before close.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
**Completion verification: PASS**

All five acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied in the implementation.

1. **`_GITIGNORE` ignores `.ticket-write.lock` and `.ticket-write.lock.d/`** — `src/rebar/_commands/init.py` lines 44–46 include `{WRITE_LOCK_NAME}`, `{MKDIR_LOCK_NAME}/`, and `{_GRAPH_CACHE_FILE}` in the f-string `_GITIGNORE`, resolving to `.ticket-write.lock`, `.ticket-write.lock.d/`, and `.graph-cache.json` respectively.

2. **Fresh `init`'d tracker shows clean `git status --porcelain`** — `test_no_untracked_runtime_artifacts_after_write_and_compile` in `tests/interfaces/store/test_lock_gitignored.py` (lines 39–54) directly asserts this: performs a write + `deps` call, confirms the lock file exists on disk, then asserts zero `??` lines in `git status --porcelain`.

3. **Ignore entries consistent with lock-name constants (no drifting literals)** — `init.py` imports `WRITE_LOCK_NAME`/`MKDIR_LOCK_NAME` from `src/rebar/_store/lock.py` and `_GRAPH_CACHE_FILE` from `src/rebar/graph/_cache.py` (lines 29–30), using them directly in the f-string; no hardcoded duplicate literals exist.

4. **Migration path for existing trackers** — `_commit_gitignore` (lines 363–381) implements an idempotent migration: when a `.gitignore` already exists on the tickets branch, it computes missing lines vs. `_GITIGNORE` and appends them in a new commit. This function is called from both the fresh-init path (line 236) and the already-initialized path (line 207).

5. **A test asserts the seeded tracker `.gitignore` ignores both lock files** — `test_seeded_gitignore_covers_lock_and_graph_cache` in `tests/interfaces/store/test_lock_gitignored.py` (lines 29–36) asserts exact full-line matches for `WRITE_LOCK_NAME`, `MKDIR_LOCK_NAME/`, and `_GRAPH_CACHE_FILE` in the committed `.gitignore`.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary
The single-locked write path uses an flock-based lock file `.ticket-write.lock` (and the mkdir lock `.ticket-write.lock.d`). Under flock, the lock file is intentionally NOT unlinked on release (deleting it would race other lockers), so it correctly persists in the tracker working tree after every write. The problem: the tracker `.gitignore` seeded by `rebar init` does not list these lock files, so they surface as untracked files in the `.tickets-tracker` working tree — noising up `git status` and tripping git-cleanliness checks / stop hooks.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Have a tracker worktree on the `tickets` branch (`.tickets-tracker/`).
2. Perform any write, e.g. `rebar comment <id> "..."`.
3. `cd .tickets-tracker && git status --porcelain`

- Expected: clean working tree (lock files ignored).
- Actual: `?? .ticket-write.lock` appears as an untracked file.

## Root cause
- Lock names are defined in `src/rebar/_store/lock.py`: `WRITE_LOCK_NAME = ".ticket-write.lock"`, `MKDIR_LOCK_NAME = ".ticket-write.lock.d"`.
- The tracker `.gitignore` is seeded from the `_GITIGNORE` constant in `src/rebar/_commands/init.py` (~line 29), which lists `.env-id`, `.closure-key`, `.state-cache`, `.scratch/`, `.cache.json`, `*/.cache.json` — but NOT the write-lock files.

## Fix
- Add `.ticket-write.lock` and `.ticket-write.lock.d/` to `_GITIGNORE` in `src/rebar/_commands/init.py`, ideally sourced from the `WRITE_LOCK_NAME` / `MKDIR_LOCK_NAME` constants in `src/rebar/_store/lock.py` so the ignore list cannot drift from the lock names.
- Existing trackers won't pick up the new entry automatically (the committed tickets `.gitignore` is seeded once in `_commit_gitignore`); define a migration (append on upgrade / `fsck` repair, or a documented one-liner).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_GITIGNORE` in `src/rebar/_commands/init.py` ignores `.ticket-write.lock` and `.ticket-write.lock.d/`.
- [ ] On a freshly `init`'d tracker, `git status --porcelain` in the tracker worktree shows no untracked lock files after any write.
- [ ] The ignore entries are kept consistent with the lock-name constants in `src/rebar/_store/lock.py` (no drifting duplicate literals).
- [ ] A migration path is defined for existing trackers.
- [ ] A test asserts the seeded tracker `.gitignore` ignores both lock files.
compiled_state.title: Tracker .gitignore omits write-lock files (.ticket-write.lock[.d]); flock lock surfaces as untracked
